title: Tangy Jamaican Almond Paste Brownies
description: Sour, chewy brownies featuring almond paste and a hint of Jamaican jerk spice, pressure‑cooked for a decadent keto-friendly treat.
instructions: Preheat the pressure cooker and line an 8x8-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
Melt the coconut oil over low heat and stir in the almond paste until fully blended.
Add the erythritol, vanilla extract, sea salt, jerk spice mix, lemon zest, and lime juice; whisk until smooth.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, then fold in the cocoa powder and almond flour until a uniform batter forms.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Place the pan in the pressure cooker, seal the lid, and set to high pressure for 15 minutes.
When the timer ends, perform a quick release of pressure and carefully remove the pan from the cooker.
Let the brownies rest in the pan for about 10 minutes to set and cool slightly.
Cut the brownies into 12 square pieces and serve warm or store in an airtight container.
Optional: dust with a little powdered erythritol or a splash of lime zest for extra zing before serving.
